member.name: M:Foundatio.Extensions.Hosting.Messaging.SQSMessageBusServiceCollectionExtensions.AddSQSMessageBus(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder})
member.summary: Adds an SQS message bus to the service collection.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Extensions.Hosting.Messaging.SQSMessageBusServiceCollectionExtensions.AddSQSMessageBus(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.String,System.Action{Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder})
member.summary: Adds an SQS message bus to the service collection using a connection string.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus
member.summary: AWS SNS/SQS message bus implementation that uses SNS (Simple Notification Service) for publishing and SQS (Simple Queue Service) for subscribing.

remarks.para: Architecture: This implementation uses the SNS fan-out pattern where messages are published to an SNS topic and delivered to SQS queues subscribed to that topic. Each subscriber gets its own SQS queue, enabling true pub/sub semantics where all subscribers receive all messages.

Queue Management: By default, each message bus instance creates a unique SQS queue with a randomly generated name. For durable subscriptions that persist across restarts, use to specify a fixed queue name.

Policy Management: When subscribing, the implementation automatically configures the SQS queue policy to allow the SNS topic to send messages. Policies are merged rather than replaced, preserving any existing permissions. This is important for durable queues that may be reused across restarts or shared between processes.

Resource Optimization: The implementation checks for existing queues and policies before creating or updating them, minimizing unnecessary AWS API calls. This is especially important in multi-process scenarios where multiple instances may start simultaneously.
Cleanup: By default, SQS queues are automatically deleted when the message bus is disposed (). For durable subscriptions, set this to false to preserve the queue.

Delayed Messages: Delayed message delivery is handled in-memory using timers from the base class, not SQS delay queues. This provides sub-second precision but means delayed messages are lost if the process restarts before delivery.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.#ctor(Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified options.
member.param: The configuration options for the message bus.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.#ctor(Foundatio.Builder{Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder,Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using a builder pattern.
member.param: A function that configures the options builder.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.SnsClient
member.summary: Gets the underlying Amazon SNS client used for publishing messages.
member.remarks: The client is lazily initialized on first access. Use this property to perform advanced SNS operations not exposed by the message bus interface.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.SqsClient
member.summary: Gets the underlying Amazon SQS client used for receiving messages.
member.remarks: The client is lazily initialized on first access. Use this property to perform advanced SQS operations not exposed by the message bus interface.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.EnsureTopicCreatedAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Ensures the SNS topic exists, creating it if necessary and permitted.
member.remarks: This method first attempts to find an existing topic by name. If not found and is true, it creates the topic. The operation is thread-safe and idempotent.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.GetTopicName(System.Type)
member.summary: Gets the topic name for a given message type using the configured TopicResolver.
member.param: The CLR type of the message.
member.returns: The resolved topic name, or the default topic if no resolver is configured or it returns null.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.GetOrCreateTopicArnAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets or creates the SNS topic ARN for the specified topic name, with memoization.
member.param: The name of the SNS topic.
member.returns: The ARN of the SNS topic.
member.remarks: Topic ARNs are cached in a ConcurrentDictionary to avoid redundant AWS API calls. Caller must hold the lock when topic creation may occur.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.CreateTopicImplAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Internal implementation of topic creation. Caller must hold the lock.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.EnsureTopicSubscriptionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Ensures an SQS queue is subscribed to the SNS topic and starts the message polling loop.

remarks.para:
This method performs the following operations:
Checks if the SQS queue already exists (to avoid redundant CreateQueue calls)
Creates the queue if it doesn't exist, with configured encryption and visibility settings
Retrieves the queue ARN and existing policy
Updates the queue policy only if the SNS topic permission is not already present
Subscribes the queue to the SNS topic with raw message delivery enabled
Starts the background message polling loop

Multi-Process Safety: The implementation is designed to minimize redundant AWS API calls when multiple processes start simultaneously. It checks for existing queues and policies before creating or updating them.

Policy Merging: Queue policies are merged rather than replaced, preserving any existing permissions. Each SNS topic gets a unique statement ID based on its ARN hash, allowing the same queue to receive messages from multiple topics if needed.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.ValidateQueueName(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a queue name conforms to SQS naming requirements.
member.param: The parameter name for exception messages.
member.exception: Thrown when the name is null, empty, longer than 80 characters, or contains invalid characters.
member.remarks: SQS queue names must be 1-80 characters and can only contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens (-), and underscores (_).

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.GetSubscriptionQueueName
member.summary: Gets the SQS queue name for this subscription.
member.returns: The configured if set, otherwise a generated name based on the topic name and a unique identifier.
member.remarks: Generated queue names follow the pattern "{Topic}-{Guid}" and are truncated to 80 characters to comply with SQS naming limits. The configured queue name is validated in the constructor.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.PolicyContainsStatement(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the existing policy already contains a statement with the specified Sid.
member.param: The existing IAM policy JSON, or null/empty if no policy exists.
The statement ID to search for.
member.returns: True if the policy contains a statement with the specified Sid; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method is used to avoid redundant SetQueueAttributes calls when the policy already contains the required SNS permission. If the policy cannot be parsed, it returns false to trigger a policy update.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBus.GetMergedQueuePolicy(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Merges a new SNS-to-SQS policy statement into an existing queue policy, or creates a new policy if none exists.
member.param: The existing IAM policy JSON, or null/empty if no policy exists.
The ARN of the SQS queue to grant access to.
The ARN of the SNS topic that will send messages.

remarks.para: This method ensures that durable queues can be reused across restarts without losing existing permissions. Each SNS topic gets a unique statement ID (Sid) based on its ARN hash, formatted as "AllowSNS-{hash}".

The merge behavior:
If no policy exists, creates a new policy with the SNS permission
If a statement with the same Sid exists, replaces it (in case the queue ARN changed)
If no matching statement exists, adds the new statement to the existing policy
This approach allows a single SQS queue to receive messages from multiple SNS topics if needed, though the typical use case is a 1-1 relationship.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.ConnectionString
member.summary: The connection string containing AWS credentials and configuration. Format: "AccessKey=xxx;SecretKey=xxx;Region=us-east-1" or "ServiceUrl=http://localhost:4566;AccessKey=xxx;SecretKey=xxx"

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.Credentials
member.summary: The AWS credentials to use for authentication. If not specified, uses default credential resolution.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.Region
member.summary: The AWS region endpoint. If not specified, uses default region resolution.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.ServiceUrl
member.summary: The service URL for LocalStack or custom endpoints. When set, overrides the region endpoint.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.CanCreateTopic
member.summary: Whether the SNS topic can be created if it doesn't exist. Default is true.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.SubscriptionQueueName
member.summary: The name of the SQS subscription queue. If not specified, a unique queue name will be generated. Use this for durable subscriptions that persist across restarts.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.SubscriptionQueueAutoDelete
member.summary: Whether the subscription queue should be automatically deleted when the message bus is disposed. Default is true for ephemeral queues, set to false for durable subscriptions.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.ReadQueueTimeout
member.summary: The timeout for reading messages from the SQS queue using long polling. Default is 20 seconds.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.DequeueInterval
member.summary: The interval between dequeue attempts when no messages are available. Default is 1 second.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.MessageVisibilityTimeout
member.summary: The visibility timeout for messages. Messages will be invisible to other consumers for this duration after being received. If not set, uses SQS default (30 seconds).

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.SqsManagedSseEnabled
member.summary: Whether to use SQS managed server-side encryption (SSE-SQS). Default is false.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.KmsMasterKeyId
member.summary: The KMS master key ID for server-side encryption (SSE-KMS).

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds
member.summary: The KMS data key reuse period in seconds. Default is 300 seconds (5 minutes).

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptions.TopicResolver
member.summary: Optional function to resolve the SNS topic name for a given message type. If not set or returns null, falls back to the default . This enables routing different message types to different SNS topics.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.ConnectionString(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the connection string containing AWS credentials and configuration.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.Credentials(Amazon.Runtime.AWSCredentials)
member.summary: Sets the AWS credentials to use for authentication.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.Credentials(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the AWS credentials using access key and secret key.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.Region(Amazon.RegionEndpoint)
member.summary: Sets the AWS region endpoint.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.Region(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the AWS region by system name (e.g., "us-east-1").

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.ServiceUrl(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the service URL for LocalStack or custom endpoints.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.CanCreateTopic(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets whether the SNS topic can be created if it doesn't exist.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.EnableCreateTopic
member.summary: Enables automatic topic creation if it doesn't exist.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.DisableCreateTopic
member.summary: Disables automatic topic creation. An exception will be thrown if the topic doesn't exist.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.SubscriptionQueueName(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the subscription queue name for durable subscriptions.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.SubscriptionQueueAutoDelete(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Configures whether the subscription queue should be auto-deleted on dispose.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.UseDurableSubscription(System.String)
member.summary: Configures a durable subscription that persists across restarts. Sets the queue name and disables auto-delete.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.ReadQueueTimeout(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Sets the timeout for reading messages from the SQS queue using long polling.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.DequeueInterval(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Sets the interval between dequeue attempts when no messages are available.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.MessageVisibilityTimeout(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Sets the visibility timeout for messages.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.UseKmsEncryption(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Enables KMS server-side encryption (SSE-KMS) with the specified master key.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.UseSqsManagedEncryption
member.summary: Enables SQS managed server-side encryption (SSE-SQS).

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.SQSMessageBusOptionsBuilder.TopicResolver(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Sets a function to resolve the SNS topic name for a given message type. This enables routing different message types to different SNS topics.
member.param: A function that takes a message type and returns the topic name. Return null to use the default topic.
